requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want Stash to automatically capture my Claude Code prompts so that my thinking process is preserved without any manual effort.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN a new message is appended to any ~/.claude/projects/*/sessions/*.jsonl file, THEN the system SHALL parse the message within 2 seconds and store it as a RawPrompt record.
WHILE the application is running, the system SHALL maintain file watchers on all active Claude Code session directories.
IF a monitored JSONL file is rotated or deleted, THEN the system SHALL detect the new file and resume watching without data loss.
The system SHALL NOT consume more than 1% CPU while idle-monitoring JSONL files.

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Rust notify crate for fs watching; JSONL parser; background thread/async task
downstream_impact.test_complexity: Medium — requires mock JSONL file writes and timing assertions

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want Stash to monitor my git activity so that project status can be inferred automatically.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN a git commit, push, or merge event is detected in a monitored project directory, THEN the system SHALL record a git signal within 5 seconds.
IF no git activity is detected for 30 minutes in a previously active context, THEN the system SHALL mark the context as potentially parked.
The system SHALL NOT execute git commands more frequently than once per 30 seconds per context.

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Git CLI subprocess calls; .git directory watching; commit log parsing

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want to manually input my current intent via a global hotkey so that I can correct or supplement auto-detected intents.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN the user presses the configured global hotkey, THEN the system SHALL display a text input dialog within 300ms.
WHEN the user submits text in the input dialog, THEN the system SHALL create a new Intent record with source='manual' and update the context's current intent.

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Global hotkey registration (X11); lightweight popup window; Tauri command

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want captured prompts to be automatically distilled into concise intent narratives so that I can quickly understand what I was thinking.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN 3-5 new RawPrompts accumulate for a context, THEN the system SHALL trigger LLM distillation and produce one narrative-tier Intent within 5 seconds.
WHILE distillation is in progress, the system SHALL display a subtle loading indicator on the affected context's bubble.
IF the LLM service is unavailable, THEN the system SHALL queue the distillation request and retry with exponential backoff (max 5 retries).
IF the distilled intent is in Chinese, THEN the system SHALL preserve Chinese text without translating.
Each narrative Intent SHALL NOT exceed 200 characters.

downstream_impact.tech_implications: LLM API call (Ollama local); prompt template; sliding window trigger; retry queue
downstream_impact.test_complexity: High — requires LLM mock, timing, queue behavior

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want older intents to be automatically compressed into summaries and labels so that my intent history stays readable over time.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN narrative intents are older than 4 hours, THEN the system SHALL batch-compress them into summary-tier intents (1-3 day granularity).
WHEN summary intents are older than 3 days, THEN the system SHALL compress them into label-tier intents (1 week+ granularity).
WHILE compression runs, the compressed_from chain SHALL be maintained so that any compressed intent can trace back to its source intents.
Compression SHALL NOT delete source intents; they SHALL be marked as archived but remain queryable.

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Periodic timer; batch LLM calls (cloud API); compressed_from FK chain in SQLite

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want Stash to detect when my thinking direction changes so that pivot points are highlighted in my intent timeline.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN a new intent is distilled, THEN the system SHALL compare it semantically with the previous intent and flag direction changes above a configurable similarity threshold.
IF a direction change is detected, THEN the system SHALL create an Intent record with is_direction_change=true and display a '转折' marker in the UI.
Direction change detection SHALL be combined with the distillation LLM call to avoid additional API overhead.

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Semantic comparison in LLM prompt; threshold config; combined API call

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want to manually correct a distilled intent so that inaccurate auto-summaries can be fixed.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN the user clicks on an intent in the card popup, THEN the system SHALL enable inline editing of the intent text.
WHEN the user submits an edited intent, THEN the system SHALL create a new Intent record with source='manual_correction' linked to the original.
Manual corrections SHALL NOT overwrite the original auto-distilled intent; both versions SHALL be preserved.

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Inline edit UI component; new intent record creation; original preservation

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want Stash to automatically detect which project I'm working on so that intents are organized by context without manual assignment.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN a new Claude Code session is detected, THEN the system SHALL map it to an existing Context or create a new one based on the project directory.
IF the project hash cannot be resolved to a directory, THEN the system SHALL create a context with name 'Unknown' and allow the user to manually assign it.
The system SHALL support a maximum of 4 active contexts simultaneously.

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Claude Code project hash reverse engineering; directory mapping; context lifecycle
downstream_impact.test_complexity: Medium — hash mapping, context creation edge cases

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want Stash to automatically detect if my project is running, done, stuck, or parked so that the pet's visual status reflects reality.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN active Claude Code prompting is detected, THEN the system SHALL set the context status to 'running'.
WHEN a git commit or push is detected after a coding session, THEN the system SHALL set the context status to 'done'.
IF repeated error patterns are detected in prompts without resolution for 10+ minutes, THEN the system SHALL set the context status to 'stuck'.
IF no activity is detected for 30+ minutes, THEN the system SHALL set the context status to 'parked'.
WHEN the user manually overrides a status via the card popup, THEN the system SHALL update the status immediately and suppress auto-detection for that context for 15 minutes.

downstream_impact.tech_implications: State machine (running/done/stuck/parked); heuristic rules; manual override with cooldown
downstream_impact.test_complexity: High — state transitions, timing, override logic

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want the pet's surrounding bubbles to visually encode context status so that I can perceive project states at a glance.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHILE a context is active, the system SHALL display a colored bubble near the pet sprite corresponding to its status: purple=running, teal=done, amber=stuck, gray=parked.
IF 1-4 contexts are active, THEN the system SHALL arrange bubbles in a ring pattern around the pet sprite without overlapping.
WHEN a context status changes, THEN the bubble color SHALL animate to the new color within 0.5 seconds.
WHILE the pet is in default state, all bubbles SHALL be semi-transparent gray to minimize visual distraction.
WHEN a status change event occurs (done/stuck), THEN the affected bubble SHALL briefly highlight (pulse animation) to attract peripheral attention.
Bubble rendering SHALL NOT exceed 2% GPU usage during animations.

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Pixel art sprite rendering; ring layout algorithm; CSS/canvas animation; GPU budget
downstream_impact.test_complexity: Medium — visual regression tests, animation timing

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want to click a bubble and jump directly to the relevant terminal or PR so that context switching is instant.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN the user clicks a context bubble, THEN the system SHALL display the card popup for that context.
WHEN the user clicks the jump button on a 'running' context card, THEN the system SHALL focus the terminal window running the associated Claude Code session.
WHEN the user clicks the jump button on a 'done' context card, THEN the system SHALL open the most recent git PR URL in the default browser.
IF the target terminal window cannot be found, THEN the system SHALL display a toast notification 'Terminal not found' and fall back to opening the project directory.
Jump-to-terminal SHALL support at minimum: GNOME Terminal, Konsole, Alacritty, and kitty on X11.

downstream_impact.tech_implications: X11 window enumeration (wmctrl/xdotool); terminal PID mapping; git remote parsing for PR URL

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want a pixel pet floating on my desktop that reflects my work state so that I have an ambient awareness companion.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHILE the application is running, the pet window SHALL be always-on-top, transparent-background, and click-through (except on the pet sprite and bubbles).
WHILE no status change is occurring, the pet sprite SHALL play a subtle idle animation (breathing/blinking) at <1% CPU.
WHEN the user drags the pet sprite, THEN the system SHALL reposition the pet window and persist the new position across restarts.
The pet window SHALL NOT intercept keyboard events or block interaction with underlying desktop windows.

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Tauri transparent window; X11 always-on-top + click-through; sprite sheet animation; position persistence
downstream_impact.test_complexity: High — X11 window behavior varies across WMs

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want a compact popup card showing current intent, status, and a jump button so that I can get details with one click.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN the user clicks a context bubble, THEN the system SHALL display a 320px-wide popup card adjacent to the bubble within 200ms.
WHILE the card is visible, it SHALL display: context name, current intent text, status badge, last-updated timestamp, and a jump action button.
WHEN the user clicks outside the card or presses Escape, THEN the card SHALL dismiss within 150ms.
The card SHALL use the pixel-art visual style consistent with the pet window design DNA.

downstream_impact.tech_implications: React popup component; Tauri window positioning; dismiss handlers

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want to view my intent evolution as a vertical timeline graph so that I can trace my thinking history over time.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN the user opens the intent graph view, THEN the system SHALL render a vertical timeline showing all intents for the selected context, most recent at top.
WHILE viewing the graph, intents SHALL be displayed at their appropriate compression tier: narrative (recent), summary (older), label (oldest).
WHEN the user clicks a compressed intent node, THEN the system SHALL expand it to show its source intents (compressed_from chain).
WHILE direction-change intents exist, they SHALL be marked with an amber '转折' pill tag on the timeline.
The graph SHALL render within 500ms for up to 200 visible intent nodes.

downstream_impact.tech_implications: React timeline component; SQLite queries with compressed_from joins; virtual scrolling for large datasets
downstream_impact.test_complexity: Medium — rendering performance, data chain integrity

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want Stash to use local LLM for realtime tasks and cloud LLM for batch compression so that I get both speed and quality.

acceptance_criteria.ears: IF the user configures 'local' LLM mode, THEN realtime distillation SHALL use Ollama with a 7-8B parameter model, and periodic compression SHALL also use local.
IF the user configures 'hybrid' LLM mode, THEN realtime distillation SHALL use Ollama local and periodic compression SHALL use the configured cloud API.
IF Ollama is not running or not installed, THEN the system SHALL display a setup guide and gracefully degrade to cloud-only mode (if configured) or queue requests.

downstream_impact.tech_implications: LLM abstraction layer; Ollama HTTP client; cloud API client; mode switching; health check
downstream_impact.test_complexity: Medium — multiple mode combinations, failover logic

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want my local Stash data to be protected with proper file permissions so that my prompt history is not readable by other users.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHILE the application creates or accesses ~/.stash/stash.db, the file permissions SHALL be set to 0600 (owner read/write only).

downstream_impact.tech_implications: File permission setting on DB creation; permission check on startup

invariants.statement: Raw prompts, once captured, SHALL NEVER be deleted or modified by the system. They are the immutable source of truth.
The number of simultaneously active contexts SHALL NEVER exceed 4.
The pet window's non-interactive areas SHALL ALWAYS be click-through, never blocking desktop interaction.
End-to-end latency from prompt capture to UI update SHALL NEVER exceed 5 seconds under normal conditions.
Total application memory usage SHALL NEVER exceed 50MB during idle operation.
No prompt content sanitization shall be performed before sending to the configured LLM. The user's LLM mode selection constitutes informed consent.
Information flow SHALL ALWAYS be Stash→User, not User→Stash. The only user inputs are: manual intent, manual correction, manual status override, and configuration.
